description: Ideas, spans of time, and events may take optional 'nophe' when counted. The same classifier can accompany the event noun supplied by any verb, as in 'ta nophe thalo', one walk. A physical referent uses one of the physical classifier categories instead.
articulatory_notes: Begin dental 'n' with the tongue apex behind upper incisors as voice passes through the nasal cavity. Keep the voice as the mouth rounds into back 'o' at true-middle height. Narrow the lip opening to make unvoiced 'ph', then spread toward 'e', unrounded at the front and true-middle in height. Accent 'no'.
sound_symbolism: Initial 'no' holds its resonance behind the dental nasal; the second syllable escapes between lightly parted lips. The shift from contained hum to outward breath can feel like an idea entering speech.
usage_notes: The order is number or 'wia', optional 'nophe', then noun. Its absence changes no count: 'wi torua' and 'wi nophe torua' are both complete. An event noun retains the form of its verb when classified.
